message:
bd-31qb: retention tiers policy + config

Add tiered retention rules to StorageConfig:
- RetentionTier struct with severity, event_type prefix, and handled filters
- First-match-wins evaluation order with global fallback
- Default tiers: critical=90d, warning=30d, info=7d
- Hot-reload support for tier changes
- 11 unit tests covering matching logic, TOML roundtrip, hot-reload

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-2z6d","title":"Remove --from-status CLI handling and IPC StatusUpdate code","description":"## Overview

With STATUS_UPDATE_LUA removed, the CLI flag \`--from-status\` and associated IPC handling become dead code. This task removes that code to clean up the codebase.

## Code to Remove

### 1. CLI Argument (crates/wa/src/main.rs)

In the Event command definition (~line 217-235):
\`\`\`rust
Event {
    #[arg(long, conflicts_with = "from_status")]
    from_uservar: bool,
    #[arg(long, conflicts_with = "from_uservar")]  // REMOVE
    from_status: bool,                              // REMOVE
    #[arg(long)]
    pane: u64,
    #[arg(long, required_if_eq("from_uservar", "true"))]
    name: Option<String>,
    #[arg(long, required_if_eq("from_uservar", "true"))]
    value: Option<String>,
    #[arg(long, required_if_eq("from_status", "true"))]  // REMOVE
    payload: Option<String>,                              // REMOVE
},
\`\`\`

After removal:
\`\`\`rust
Event {
    #[arg(long)]
    from_uservar: bool,
    #[arg(long)]
    pane: u64,
    #[arg(long, required_if_eq("from_uservar", "true"))]
    name: Option<String>,
    #[arg(long, required_if_eq("from_uservar", "true"))]
    value: Option<String>,
},
\`\`\`

### 2. CLI Handler (crates/wa/src/main.rs)

In the event command handler (~lines 6067-6110), remove the status update branch:
\`\`\`rust
if from_status {
    // ... status update handling
    // REMOVE THIS ENTIRE BLOCK
}
\`\`\`

### 3. IPC Types (crates/wa-core/src/ipc.rs)

Remove StatusUpdate from IpcRequest enum:
\`\`\`rust
pub enum IpcRequest {
    // ... other variants
    StatusUpdate(StatusUpdate),  // REMOVE
}
\`\`\`

Remove StatusUpdate struct (~lines 176-200):
\`\`\`rust
pub struct StatusUpdate {
    pub schema_version: u8,
    pub pane_id: u64,
    pub domain: String,
    // ... REMOVE ENTIRE STRUCT
}
\`\`\`

### 4. IPC Server Handler (crates/wa-core/src/ipc.rs)

Remove StatusUpdate arm in request handler (~line 504):
\`\`\`rust
IpcRequest::StatusUpdate(update) => {
    // ... REMOVE THIS MATCH ARM
}
\`\`\`

### 5. IpcClient Method (crates/wa-core/src/ipc.rs)

Remove send_status_update method (~line 774):
\`\`\`rust
pub async fn send_status_update(
    &self,
    update: StatusUpdate,
) -> Result<IpcResponse, UserVarError> {
    // REMOVE THIS METHOD
}
\`\`\`

### 6. Tests

Remove or update tests that use status update functionality:
- Any test in ipc.rs that tests StatusUpdate
- E2E test scenario "status_update" (handled in separate bead)

## Considerations

### Keep or Remove?

**Arguments for REMOVAL (recommended):**
- Dead code is confusing and adds maintenance burden
- Clear signal that this functionality is gone
- Smaller binary size
- Simpler codebase

**Arguments for KEEPING (with deprecation):**
- Users might have scripts calling \`wa event --from-status\`
- Could log warning instead of removing

**Recommendation**: Remove with clear documentation. Anyone calling this API directly (unlikely) can adapt.

### Deprecation Period?

Since this was an internal implementation detail (Lua snippet → CLI → IPC), not a public API, no deprecation period needed. The Lua snippet removal (previous bead) is the user-facing change.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] \`wa event --from-status\` no longer accepted
- [ ] StatusUpdate removed from IpcRequest enum
- [ ] StatusUpdate struct removed
- [ ] IPC server no longer handles StatusUpdate
- [ ] IpcClient::send_status_update removed
- [ ] All tests pass
- [ ] \`cargo clippy\` shows no dead code warnings

## Dependencies

- Depends on: bd-30gj (STATUS_UPDATE_LUA removed from setup.rs)

## Files to Modify

- crates/wa/src/main.rs:
  - Event command args
  - Event command handler
- crates/wa-core/src/ipc.rs:
  - IpcRequest enum
  - StatusUpdate struct
  - Server handler
  - IpcClient method
- crates/wa-core/tests/: Any StatusUpdate tests

## References

- Original implementation: Previous conversation (wa-4vx.2.7.3)

 {"id":"bd-30gj","title":"Remove STATUS_UPDATE_LUA from setup.rs (with deprecation path)","description":"## Overview

Remove the STATUS_UPDATE_LUA constant and its injection into wezterm.lua from crates/wa-core/src/setup.rs. This is the core change that eliminates the Lua performance bottleneck.

## Current State

In setup.rs (lines 54-105), STATUS_UPDATE_LUA is defined:
\`\`\`rust
const STATUS_UPDATE_LUA: &str = r#"-- Forward pane status updates to wa daemon (rate-limited)
local wa_last_status_update = {}
local WA_STATUS_UPDATE_INTERVAL_MS = 2000

wezterm.on('update-status', function(window, pane)
  // ... 50+ lines of Lua code
end)"#;
\`\`\`

This is injected via:
- \`create_wa_block()\` (line 837-838) — combines USERVAR_FORWARDING_LUA + STATUS_UPDATE_LUA
- \`generate_ssh_domains_lua()\` (lines 708-711) — appends both snippets

## Changes Required

### 1. Remove STATUS_UPDATE_LUA Constant
Delete the entire constant definition (lines 49-105).

### 2. Update create_wa_block()
Before:
\`\`\`rust
fn create_wa_block() -> String {
    format!("{WA_BEGIN_MARKER}\\n{USERVAR_FORWARDING_LUA}\\n\\n{STATUS_UPDATE_LUA}\\n{WA_END_MARKER}")
}
\`\`\`

After:
\`\`\`rust
fn create_wa_block() -> String {
    format!("{WA_BEGIN_MARKER}\\n{USERVAR_FORWARDING_LUA}\\n{WA_END_MARKER}")
}
\`\`\`

### 3. Update generate_ssh_domains_lua()
Remove STATUS_UPDATE_LUA from the generated block:
\`\`\`rust
// Line 708-711, remove:
output.push_str(STATUS_UPDATE_LUA);
output.push('\\n');
\`\`\`

### 4. Update Test Assertions
Several tests check for STATUS_UPDATE_LUA content:
- test_create_wa_block_format() — update assertions
- test_generate_ssh_domains_block_includes_hosts_and_snippets() — remove STATUS_UPDATE_LUA assertion

### 5. Migration Path for Existing Users

Users who already ran \`wa setup\` have STATUS_UPDATE_LUA in their wezterm.lua. Options:

**Option A: Silent removal on next setup**
- \`wa setup\` replaces the entire WA-managed block
- Old STATUS_UPDATE_LUA code gets removed automatically
- Simplest, but users don't know what changed

**Option B: Explicit migration command (Recommended)**
- Add \`wa setup --upgrade\` or \`wa setup migrate\`
- Shows diff of what will change
- Requires user confirmation
- Document in release notes

**Option C: Deprecation warning**
- Keep STATUS_UPDATE_LUA for one version cycle
- Print deprecation warning on \`wa watch\` startup if status updates detected
- Remove in next major version

**Recommendation**: Implement Option B with clear messaging.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] STATUS_UPDATE_LUA constant removed from setup.rs
- [ ] create_wa_block() only includes USERVAR_FORWARDING_LUA
- [ ] generate_ssh_domains_lua() only includes USERVAR_FORWARDING_LUA
- [ ] All tests updated and passing
- [ ] \`wa setup\` cleanly upgrades existing configs
- [ ] Documentation updated (CHANGELOG, migration notes)

## Backward Compatibility

- Existing wezterm.lua files with STATUS_UPDATE_LUA will continue to work
- They'll just be wasteful (sending events wa ignores)
- Running \`wa setup\` again will remove the old code

## Files to Modify

- crates/wa-core/src/setup.rs:
  - Remove STATUS_UPDATE_LUA constant (lines 49-105)
  - Update create_wa_block() (line 837)
  - Update generate_ssh_domains_lua() (lines 708-711)
- crates/wa-core/src/setup.rs tests:
  - test_create_wa_block_format()
  - test_generate_ssh_domains_block_includes_hosts_and_snippets()

## Dependencies

- Depends on: bd-jfkk (ingest loop provides all metadata)
- Depends on: bd-2v4t (alt-screen detection works)

## References

 {"id":"bd-33v2","title":"[EPIC] Audit log streaming + tailing","description":"## Background
Audit logs exist but are hard to consume in real time or integrate with external tooling.

## Goals
- Provide a streaming/tailing interface for audit logs
- Offer JSONL output for external ingestion (SIEM/log shipper)
- Ensure redaction and rate limiting

## Non-Goals
- External log shipping agent

## Considerations
- Streaming must not block writer threads
- Output must remain data-only (no ANSI)

run_scenario_alt_screen_detection() {
    local scenario_dir="$1"
    local wait_timeout="${TEST_WAIT_TIMEOUT:-30}"
    
    log_step "Starting wa watch..."
    wa watch --foreground > "$scenario_dir/wa_watch.log" 2>&1 &
    local wa_pid=$!
    sleep 2
    
    log_step "Spawning test pane..."

    sleep 1
    
    log_step "Verifying initial state (not alt-screen)..."

    if [[ "$is_alt" != "false" ]]; then
        log_fail "Expected is_alt_screen=false initially, got: $is_alt"
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_step "Sending alt-screen enter sequence..."
    # ESC [ ? 1049 h
    printf '\e[?1049h' | wezterm cli send-text --pane-id "$pane_id" --no-paste
    sleep 0.5  # Allow detection
    
    log_step "Verifying alt-screen detected..."
    state=$(wa robot state --format json)
    is_alt=$(echo "$state" | jq -r ".data.panes[] | select(.pane_id == $pane_id) | .is_alt_screen")
    if [[ "$is_alt" != "true" ]]; then
        log_fail "Expected is_alt_screen=true after ESC[?1049h, got: $is_alt"
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_step "Sending alt-screen leave sequence..."
    # ESC [ ? 1049 l
    printf '\e[?1049l' | wezterm cli send-text --pane-id "$pane_id" --no-paste
    sleep 0.5
    
    log_step "Verifying alt-screen exited..."
    state=$(wa robot state --format json)
    is_alt=$(echo "$state" | jq -r ".data.panes[] | select(.pane_id == $pane_id) | .is_alt_screen")
    if [[ "$is_alt" != "false" ]]; then
        log_fail "Expected is_alt_screen=false after ESC[?1049l, got: $is_alt"
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_pass "Alt-screen detection via escape sequences works!"
    cleanup_pane "$pane_id"
    kill "$wa_pid" 2>/dev/null
}

 {"id":"bd-sx55","title":"Implement wa-side native event listener for vendored WezTerm events","description":"## Overview

Implement the wa-side Unix socket listener that receives events from vendored WezTerm's native integration. This replaces the Lua→CLI→IPC path with direct socket communication.

## Prerequisites

- Completed: bd-3gon (Protocol design)
- Completed: bd-20fw (WezTerm fork emitting events)

## Implementation

### 1. Event Listener Module

Create \`crates/wa-core/src/native_events.rs\`:

\`\`\`rust
use std::path::PathBuf;
use tokio::net::{UnixListener, UnixStream};
use tokio::sync::broadcast;

/// Events received from native WezTerm integration.
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub enum NativeEvent {
    PaneOutput {
        pane_id: u64,
        data: Vec<u8>,
        timestamp: u64,
    },
    StateChange {
        pane_id: u64,
        title: String,
        rows: u16,
        cols: u16,
        is_alt_screen: bool,
        timestamp: u64,
    },
    UserVarChanged {
        pane_id: u64,
        name: String,
        value: String,
        timestamp: u64,
    },
    PaneCreated {
        pane_id: u64,
        domain: String,
        cwd: Option<String>,
        timestamp: u64,
    },
    PaneDestroyed {
        pane_id: u64,
        timestamp: u64,
    },
}

/// Listener for native WezTerm events.
pub struct NativeEventListener {
    socket_path: PathBuf,
    listener: UnixListener,
    event_tx: broadcast::Sender<NativeEvent>,
}

impl NativeEventListener {
    /// Create and bind the event listener socket.
    pub async fn bind(socket_path: PathBuf) -> Result<Self, Error> {
        // Remove stale socket if exists
        let _ = std::fs::remove_file(&socket_path);
        
        // Create parent directory
        if let Some(parent) = socket_path.parent() {
            std::fs::create_dir_all(parent)?;
        }
        
        let listener = UnixListener::bind(&socket_path)?;
        let (event_tx, _) = broadcast::channel(1024);
        
        Ok(Self {
            socket_path,
            listener,
            event_tx,
        })
    }
    
    /// Subscribe to events.
    pub fn subscribe(&self) -> broadcast::Receiver<NativeEvent> {
        self.event_tx.subscribe()
    }
    
    /// Run the event listener loop.
    pub async fn run(&self) -> Result<(), Error> {
        loop {
            let (stream, _) = self.listener.accept().await?;
            let event_tx = self.event_tx.clone();
            
            tokio::spawn(async move {
                if let Err(e) = handle_connection(stream, event_tx).await {
                    tracing::warn!("Native event connection error: {}", e);
                }
            });
        }
    }
}

async fn handle_connection(
    stream: UnixStream,
    event_tx: broadcast::Sender<NativeEvent>,
) -> Result<(), Error> {
    use tokio::io::{AsyncBufReadExt, BufReader};
    
    let reader = BufReader::new(stream);
    let mut lines = reader.lines();
    
    while let Some(line) = lines.next_line().await? {
        match parse_event(&line) {
            Ok(event) => {
                let _ = event_tx.send(event);  // Ignore if no subscribers
            }
            Err(e) => {
                tracing::debug!("Failed to parse native event: {}", e);
            }
        }
    }
    
    Ok(())
}

fn parse_event(json: &str) -> Result<NativeEvent, Error> {
    let value: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_str(json)?;
    
    match value.get("type").and_then(|t| t.as_str()) {
        Some("pane_output") => Ok(NativeEvent::PaneOutput {
            pane_id: value["pane_id"].as_u64().unwrap_or(0),
            data: base64::decode(value["data"].as_str().unwrap_or(""))?,
            timestamp: value["ts"].as_u64().unwrap_or(0),
        }),
        Some("state_change") => Ok(NativeEvent::StateChange {
            pane_id: value["pane_id"].as_u64().unwrap_or(0),
            title: value["state"]["title"].as_str().unwrap_or("").to_string(),
            rows: value["state"]["rows"].as_u64().unwrap_or(24) as u16,

### 2. Integration with Watcher

Modify \`crates/wa-core/src/watcher.rs\` or equivalent:

\`\`\`rust
impl Watcher {
    pub async fn run_with_native_events(&mut self) -> Result<()> {
        let native_listener = NativeEventListener::bind(
            self.config.native_event_socket.clone()
        ).await?;
        
        let mut native_rx = native_listener.subscribe();
        
        // Spawn listener task
        let listener_handle = tokio::spawn(async move {
            native_listener.run().await
        });
        
        loop {
            tokio::select! {
                // Existing poll loop
                _ = self.poll_interval.tick() => {
                    self.poll_panes().await?;
                }
                
                // Native events
                Ok(event) = native_rx.recv() => {
                    self.handle_native_event(event).await?;
                }
            }
        }
    }
    
    async fn handle_native_event(&mut self, event: NativeEvent) -> Result<()> {
        match event {
            NativeEvent::PaneOutput { pane_id, data, .. } => {
                // Feed to pattern detection
                self.pattern_engine.process(&data);
                // Feed to screen state tracker
                self.screen_tracker.process_output(pane_id, &data);
            }
            NativeEvent::StateChange { pane_id, is_alt_screen, .. } => {
                // Update pane state directly (no polling needed)
                self.pane_states.update_alt_screen(pane_id, is_alt_screen);
            }
            NativeEvent::UserVarChanged { pane_id, name, value, .. } => {
                // Same handling as IPC user-var events
                self.handle_user_var(pane_id, &name, &value).await?;
            }
            // ... other events
        }
        Ok(())
    }
}
\`\`\`

### 3. Feature Flag

Gate native event support behind a feature:

\`\`\`toml
# crates/wa-core/Cargo.toml
[features]
native-wezterm = []
\`\`\`

\`\`\`rust
#[cfg(feature = "native-wezterm")]
mod native_events;
\`\`\`

### 4. Configuration

Add config option:

\`\`\`toml
# wa.toml
[native]
enabled = true
socket_path = "/tmp/wa/events.sock"

 {"id":"wa-01x","title":"Version compatibility check: detect incompatible wa/database combinations","description":"# Task: Version Compatibility Check

## Goal
Prevent users from accidentally running incompatible wa versions against their database.

## Scenarios
1. **Newer wa, older DB**: Migration needed (usually safe)
2. **Older wa, newer DB**: DANGEROUS - wa cannot read newer schema
3. **Major version jump**: May require manual intervention

## Implementation

### Database Version Tracking
Track schema_version, min_compatible_wa, created_by_wa, created_at in wa_meta table.

### Startup Check
On open, verify:
- current_wa >= min_compatible_wa (else: WaTooOld error)
- schema_version <= supported_schema (else: SchemaTooNew error)
- schema_version == supported_schema (else: MigrationNeeded)

### User-Facing Messages
Old wa + new DB: Clear error with upgrade instructions
New wa + old DB: Offer migration with backup

## Testing
- Unit tests: version comparison logic
- Integration: simulate version mismatches
- E2E: upgrade/downgrade scenarios

## Acceptance Criteria
- Old wa refuses to open newer DB with clear error
- New wa offers migration for older DB
- Version info tracked in database metadata
- Helpful upgrade instructions provided","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T19:58:00.500288986Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-27T17:41:55.706395892Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T17:41:55.705890700Z","close_reason":"Added wa_meta version compatibility checks + schema gating","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-cli","area-storage","area-tests","area-ux","phase-1","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-01x","depends_on_id":"bd-35zb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:23Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-01x","depends_on_id":"bd-u194","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:23Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-01x","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:23Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-01x","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.2.11","type":"relates-to","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:23Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-01x","depends_on_id":"wa-rnf.5","type":"relates-to","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:23Z","created_by":"import"}]}
